The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) passed the House and is now moving to the Senate, where it is expected to use a 'current policy' baseline. The Senate Finance Committee has proposed extending and modifying key provisions of the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA), which aligns with the 'current policy' baseline approach. The bill is scheduled to be debated and passed by the Senate before the end of the 2025 fiscal year.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), signed into law on July 4, 2025, introduces new domestic content requirements for Section 48E credits, which are harmonized with Section 45Y. These requirements mandate that facilities beginning construction after June 16, 2025, must meet certain domestic content thresholds to qualify for the 45Y Production Tax Credit.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) introduced in July 2025 shortens the deadlines for the 45V hydrogen production credit and phases out the 45X advanced manufacturing production credit for critical minerals. It also introduces stricter FEOC restrictions, but does not make the existing domestic content requirements mandatory for the Investment Tax Credits (ITC) described under 48E.

The House of Representatives passed H.R. 1, the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act,' on May 22, 2025, which significantly amends the clean energy provisions of the Inflation Reduction Act. The bill accelerates the phase-out of the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X) and introduces new foreign-entity-of-concern (FEOC) restrictions, including disallowing credits to taxpayers that are a 'specified foreign entity' for tax years beginning after enactment. This suggests that the use of domestic materials or subcomponents may become a requirement.
